Scenario.title: Ransomware Attack Scenario in Communications Sector
Scenario.overview: Telecommunications companies or service providers. Simple [A targeted ransomware campaign leveraging simple attack phases to compromise systems and extort victims through encrypted file demands.]

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Financial gain through ransom payments and potential espionage motives targeting sensitive communication infrastructure.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Attack PC via USB Connection, File Creation (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: An adversary gains unauthorized access to vulnerable remote services hosted on external systems, potentially exploiting weaknesses such as default credentials or unpatched software to disrupt operations and establish a foothold within the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1427: Attack PC via USB Connection
stage.description: After compromising the initial entry point, the attacker deploys a malicious payload by inserting a compromised USB drive into endpoint devices, allowing automated execution of ransomware scripts that propagate malware across the affected workstations.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-636: Hiding Malicious Data or Code within Files
stage.description: The adversary encrypts user-accessible files using the AvosLocker algorithm, rendering data inaccessible and demanding payment for decryption keys, thereby causing significant operational disruption and extortion attempts within the communications sector.

Scenario.summary: [This simple ransomware attack targets communications firms by initially breaching internet-facing systems, then spreading via USB devices to encrypt critical files, leading to high-profile disruptions and financial losses due to lack of advanced defenses.]
